commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Human review is needed because the branch is currently dirty and the remaining decision is compatibility/message-delivery acceptance, not a narrow automated code repair.; Cleared: No dependency, workflow, credential, or supply-chain changes were found; the durable progress exposure is opt-in and documented. (sha=0e889ce683270fa99968abd2507bef444da6bcb9)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a default-off Telegram `streaming.progress.persistProgress` mode that freezes completed progress lines and prepends them above the final answer, with schema/type/UI/docs/generated metadata and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +104, Tests +189, Docs +1, Generated 0. Total +294 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is an opt-in feature PR rather than a bug report. The contributor supplied Telegram Desktop before/after proof for the changed behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human review is needed because the branch is currently dirty and the remaining decision is compatibility/message-delivery acceptance, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: feat(streaming): opt-in persistProgress mode for tool+commentary lines
target.body:
## Summary

- Add `streaming.progress.persistProgress` config flag (opt-in, default false)
- When enabled, completed tool+commentary progress lines are frozen and prepended above the final answer instead of being wiped
- Shared infra change in `channel-streaming.ts` and `progress-draft-compositor.ts` benefits all channels
- Telegram: respects 4096 char limit, truncates oldest lines, `---` divider between progress and answer

## Motivation

The P2 commentary draft system (#87072) is transient by design -- progress lines are wiped when the answer starts streaming. This PR adds an opt-in mode to persist the chronological tool+commentary record in the final message.

Builds on #87072 (merged) and pairs with #89834 (Claude CLI commentary events).

## Files changed

- `src/config/types.base.ts` -- `persistProgress` on shared `ChannelStreamingProgressConfig`
- `src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts` -- schema entry
- `src/channels/streaming.ts` -- `resolveChannelStreamingProgressPersist` + persist mode in `mergeChannelProgressDraftLine`
- `src/channels/progress-draft-compositor.ts` -- frozen line tracking + `formatFrozenProgressText(charBudget)`
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` -- prepend frozen progress to final answer
- `extensions/telegram/src/config-ui-hints.ts` -- UI hint
- `extensions/discord/src/config-ui-hints.ts` -- UI hint

## Test plan

- [x] 3 tests in `channel-streaming.test.ts` (resolver, persist stacking, default unchanged)
- [x] 4 tests in `progress-draft-compositor.test.ts` (freeze on suppress, no freeze when off, char budget, empty when off)
- [x] Existing tests pass


---

## Real behavior proof

Verified end-to-end in native Telegram Desktop (claude-cli backend) via a local crabbox capture. Same prompt ("read three files, one line each on what they do"), only `streaming.progress.persistProgress` toggled.

| `persistProgress: false` (progress wiped) | `persistProgress: true` (progress frozen above answer) |
|---|---|
| ![off](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-assets-89850/off.png) | ![on](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-assets-89850/on.png) |

Motion: [off GIF](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-assets-89850/off-motion.gif) · [on GIF](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-assets-89850/on-motion.gif). With the flag on, the `📖 Read` tool-progress lines are frozen and prepended above the final answer; with it off they are wiped and only the answer remains.

### Config scope (addresses the shared-schema concern)

`persistProgress` was added to the shared `ChannelStreamingProgress` schema/type, so Discord/Slack configs also accepted a setting only Telegram honors. It is now moved to a Telegram-only `TelegramStreamingProgressSchema`/`TelegramStreamingProgressConfig`. Generated channel-config metadata: **discord → absent, telegram → present, slack → absent**. (The bundled metadata, previously stale, is regenerated — `config:channels:check` now passes.)

### 4096-char truncation path

`formatFrozenProgressText(charBudget)` slices the **oldest** frozen lines until the formatted progress fits the budget, preserving the answer. Covered by `progress-draft-compositor.test.ts` → *"formats frozen progress text with char budget truncation"*. (Triggering the trim live needs dozens of tool calls so progress alone nears 4096; the synthetic unit test is the rigorous coverage. Also log-evidenced: a long answer + 6 progress lines exceeded 4096 and chunked into 2 messages.)

Tests/checks: `progress-draft-compositor.test.ts` 13/13 · `cli-output.test.ts` 26/26 · `config:channels:check` clean · `build:docker` green · docs updated in `docs/channels/telegram.md`.
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
